- Canada school shooting, Cyclone Gezani comparison, Tractor FC playoff opponent, Bangladesh opposition, rail sabotage, Navalny toxin, Pakistan bomb district, Sudan market strike, Ronda Rousey opponent, Israel bobsleigh broadcaster, Benfica racist-remark player, Dutch AI-tool case, and most future Iran-conflict incident markets had no Feb. 8 direct naming signal strong enough for additional changes.

## 2026-02-09 Update

Resolution events:
- Fatal secondary-school mass-shooting country resolved to Canada, matching the lead.
- Egypt planning minister resolved to Ahmed Rostom. I missed by anchoring too hard on the incumbent Rania Al-Mashat from older cabinet reporting. Lesson: reshuffle questions can resolve to obscure new appointees; leave more room for undercovered technocrats.
- Late-February U.S. address tensions country resolved to Iran, matching yesterday's update.
- Canada school-shooting town resolved to Tumbler Ridge. I missed by clustering around better-known Canadian school-violence locations. Lesson: Canadian exact-location questions need rural/small-town British Columbia and prairie tails.
- Cyclone Gezani comparator resolved to Geralda. I missed by overweighting recent/high-profile Madagascar cyclones such as Freddy and Gafilo. Lesson: "rivals" language can point to older historical benchmark storms.

Updates made:
- Bangladesh main opposition: Feb. 9 campaign reporting says BNP remains the frontrunner, while Jamaat chief Shafiqur Rahman is Tarique Rahman's main rival and leads an Islamist coalition allied with NCP. Kept Jamaat in front but raised BNP to reflect the close contest.
- Pakistan T20 markets: Feb. 9 Hindustan Times says Pakistan ended the India-match boycott and will next play the USA on Feb. 10. Reduced Pakistan's former-champion failure risk and trimmed the India tail for Pakistan's clinching-win opponent, while keeping Namibia as the most likely final clincher.
- Deranque city: a resolved linked question explicitly described the "Lyon event" outside which Quentin Deranque was attacked. Raised Lyon sharply for the active city question.
- Deranque ambassador: because the linked city question frames a Macron-Meloni dispute, shifted the lead from the older Charles Kushner antisemitism-comments precedent toward Italy's ambassador to France, Emanuela D'Alessandro, while keeping Kushner as a meaningful tail.
- Navalny toxin: older but directly relevant sources repeatedly identify Novichok as the confirmed toxin in Navalny samples by European labs. Raised Novichok from 60% to 78%.

Watched but no update:
- Tractor FC playoff opponent, aircraft-carrier joiner, Olympic rail sabotage, Bangladesh cabinet woman, Ronda Rousey opponent, Israel bobsleigh broadcaster, Benfica racist-remark player, Russia military-police explosion town, Iranian frigate, France-US repost source, South Africa T20 captain, Lebanon command-centre group and US-Iran venue questions had no Feb. 9 naming signal strong enough for additional changes.

## 2026-02-10 Update

Resolution events:
- No new Feb. 10 resolution events were present; the resolved count remained at 150.

Updates made:
- Second Middle East carrier: Feb. 10 Independent/Reuters reporting says Trump is considering sending a second carrier and names USS George Washington and USS George H.W. Bush as the most probable candidates, with USS Gerald R. Ford also possible from the Caribbean. Replaced the stale Carl Vinson/Ford-led forecast with George Washington and George H.W. Bush as the top two.
- Pakistan T20 markets: Al Jazeera reports Pakistan beat the USA by 32 runs for its second win in two games. Reduced the chance Pakistan is the former champion that misses the Super Eight and cut the USA as the clinching-win opponent, while raising Namibia as the likely final clincher.
- Olympic rail sabotage: the Feb. 7 Reuters/Straits Times wording explicitly says the fire targeted the line between Bologna and Venice, and the Feb. 10 anarchist-claim story did not change that geography. Raised Bologna and Venice.
- Tractor FC AFC playoff: Feb. 8 Reuters/Straits Times says Al-Hilal, Al-Ahli, Tractor and Al-Wahda had already booked last-16 places, with Al-Ittihad, Al-Duhail and Sharjah fighting for remaining west spots. Broadened the forecast away from the thin Al Nassr/Al Hilal prior toward the qualified and near-qualified west clubs.
- US-Iran talks venues: Feb. 10 coverage again places active mediation in Oman, with Ali Larijani meeting the Omani sultan and then travelling to Qatar. Raised Oman/Muscat and kept Doha/Qatar as the main alternate.

Watched but no update:
- Bangladesh opposition, Navalny toxin, Deranque city/ambassador, Sudan market strike town, Pakistan motorcycle-bomb district, Ronda Rousey opponent, Israel bobsleigh broadcaster, Benfica racist-remark player, Dutch AI-tool court case, Afghan air-raid provinces, Iranian frigate and Winter Olympics medal/hockey questions had no Feb. 10 signal strong enough for additional changes.

## 2026-02-11 Update

Resolution events:
- Tractor FC playoff opponent resolved to Shabab Al-Ahli. I missed after broadening to several west-region clubs but still omitted Shabab Al-Ahli, even though the Feb. 8 Reuters preview named them among teams vying for the top eight. Lesson: when a preview lists active contenders for a bracket slot, include the named contender even if it is not one of the glamour clubs.

Updates made:
- Bangladesh main opposition: Hindustan Times/Reuters coverage says Bangladesh votes on Feb. 12, with the main contest between BNP- and Jamaat-led coalitions and polls showing a BNP advantage. Raised Jamaat because a BNP win makes Jamaat the likely main opposition, while keeping BNP as the alternate if Jamaat overperforms.
- Second Middle East carrier: Feb. 11 Reuters/Korea Herald repeats that USS George Washington and USS George H.W. Bush are the most likely second-carrier candidates, while Hindustan Times says the Bush has left Norfolk and Ford remains in the Caribbean. Shifted the lead slightly to USS George H.W. Bush, with George Washington close behind.
- Deranque city, Navalny toxin, and Bologna-Venice rail section: raised confidence on the already-strong linked evidence as their resolution dates approach.
- Pakistan motorcycle-bomb district: a Feb. 11 Khaleej Times article places a fresh police attack on the outskirts of Dera Ismail Khan, near Waziristan. Added Dera Ismail Khan as lead for the northwest Pakistan police-station bomb question, while keeping Bannu and North Waziristan tails.
- T20 markets: Pakistan remains top of Group A after beating the USA, while Australia are missing Marsh/Tim David plus the Cummins-Hazlewood-Starc pace core. Raised Australia failure risk, added Ireland as a live alternate for Pakistan's Super Eights opener, and broadened Super Eights Group 1 leader to include Pakistan and South Africa.
- US-Iran talks venues: Larijani travelled from Oman to Qatar, and reporting emphasized Qatar's role as a past mediator. Trimmed Oman/Muscat slightly and raised Qatar/Doha as the main alternate.

Watched but no update:
- Sudan market strike town, Bangladesh cabinet woman, Ronda Rousey opponent, Israel bobsleigh broadcaster, Benfica racist-remark player, Democratic SOTU response, Dutch AI-tool case, Iranian frigate, Lebanon command-centre group, South Africa T20 captain, France-US repost source and Winter Olympics medal/hockey questions had no Feb. 11 signal strong enough for additional changes.

## 2026-02-12 Update

Resolution events:
- Bangladesh main opposition resolved to Jamaat-e-Islami, matching the lead. The BNP-win/Jamaat-opposition read was correct.
- Second Middle East carrier resolved to Gerald R Ford. I kept Ford only as a tail and overweighted George H.W. Bush/George Washington after the Reuters candidate list. Lesson: when an already-deployed carrier is named as a possible option from a nearby theater, keep more mass on the operationally available ship even if press says other candidates are "most likely".
- Deranque city resolved to Lyon, matching the high-confidence linked-event read.

Updates made:
- Dutch AI-tool court case: older UK/French/Indonesian reporting and Feb. 12 xAI coverage keep pointing to Grok as the image tool under explicit/deepfake scrutiny. Raised Grok and trimmed generic image-model alternatives.
- Bangladesh cabinet woman: election-day reporting says the BNP is the frontrunner, Jamaat is its main rival, and Jamaat fielded no women candidates. Added BNP women Rumeen Farhana, Selima Rahman and Shama Obaid while retaining Sharmeen S Murshid as a technocratic/interim-continuity possibility.
- T20 markets: India beat Namibia by 93 runs and now top Group A on net run rate, Sri Lanka crushed Oman for a second win, and England's loss to West Indies leaves them needing two wins. Shifted the former-champion failure risk toward Australia/England and away from Sri Lanka; made Sri Lanka the slight lead for Pakistan's Super Eights opener; added West Indies as a live Super Eights Group 1 leader candidate.
- Pakistan clinching opponent: India and Pakistan both have two wins, but Namibia are 0-2 after losses to the Netherlands and India. Kept Namibia as the lead final-clincher, cut the stale USA mass, and added a modest India tail in case Pakistan clinches by beating India.
- Navalny toxin and Olympic rail: with no contrary Feb. 12 signal and direct prior reporting still intact, raised Novichok and Bologna-Venice slightly before their near-term resolution dates.

Watched but no update:
- Sudan market strike town, northwest Pakistan motorcycle-bomb district, Ronda Rousey opponent, Israel bobsleigh broadcaster, Storm Marta/Leonardo, Benfica racist-remark player, Russia military-police explosion town, Iranian frigate, Democratic SOTU response, France-US repost source, Lebanon command-centre group and South Africa T20 captain had no Feb. 12 naming signal strong enough for additional changes.

('sudan_market', r'Sudan|drone strike|market|El Obeid|Bara|North Kordofan|Omdurman|Darfur'),
('bangladesh_cabinet', r'Bangladesh|BNP|Jamaat|cabinet|minister|Rumeen|Selima|Shama|Sharmeen|Rumana|Tarique'),
('ronda', r'Ronda Rousey|Holly Holm|Kayla Harrison|MMA return|UFC'),
('storm', r'Storm Marta|Storm Leonardo|flooding in France|Western Europe|storm'),
('bobsleigh', r'Israel.*bobsleigh|bobsleigh.*Israel|commentary segment|BBC|CBC'),
('benfica', r'Benfica|Vinicius|Real Madrid|racist remark|Otamendi|Florentino'),
('russia_explosion', r'military police|explosion.*St Petersburg|Gatchina|Vsevolozhsk'),
('iran_frigate', r'Iranian frigate|frigate.*Sri Lanka|Dena|Sahand'),
('dem_response', r'Democratic response|State of the Union|Gretchen Whitmer|Andy Beshear|Hakeem Jeffries'),
('grok_ai', r'Grok|deepfake|sexual imagery|Dutch court|Netherlands.*AI')]
for label,pat in patterns:
    rx=re.compile(pat,re.I)
    hits=[]
    with path.open(errors='ignore') as f:
        for i,line in enumerate(f,1):
            try: a=json.loads(line)
            except: continue
            dp=str(a.get('date_publish') or '')[:10]
            if dp and dp>'2026-02-13': continue
            blob=(a.get('title') or '')+'\\n'+(a.get('content') or '')
            if rx.search(blob):
                hits.append((i,dp,a.get('source'),a.get('title')))
                if len(hits)>=12: break
    print('\\n--',label,len(hits),'--')
    for h in hits: print(h)
PY"

/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import json, pathlib, re
path=pathlib.Path('articles/2026/02/13/articles.jsonl')
patterns=[
('pakistan_cricket', r'T20 World Cup|Pakistan|India|Namibia|Netherlands|USA|Group A|Super Eight|Australia|Sri Lanka|England|West Indies|Italy|Zimbabwe|Ireland|Oman'),
('pakistan_bomb', r'motorcycle bomb|police station|Dera Ismail Khan|Bannu|North Waziristan|Lakki Marwat|Peshawar|Khyber Pakhtunkhwa|northwest Pakistan'),
('sudan_market', r'Sudan|drone strike|market|El Obeid|Bara|North Kordofan|Omdurman|Darfur'),
('bangladesh_cabinet', r'Bangladesh|BNP|Jamaat|cabinet|minister|Rumeen|Selima|Shama|Sharmeen|Rumana|Tarique'),
('ronda', r'Ronda Rousey|Holly Holm|Kayla Harrison|MMA return|UFC'),
('storm', r'Storm Marta|Storm Leonardo|flooding in France|Western Europe|storm'),
('bobsleigh', r'Israel.*bobsleigh|bobsleigh.*Israel|commentary segment|BBC|CBC'),
('benfica', r'Benfica|Vinicius|Real Madrid|racist remark|Otamendi|Florentino'),
('russia_explosion', r'military police|explosion.*St Petersburg|Gatchina|Vsevolozhsk'),
('iran_frigate', r'Iranian frigate|frigate.*Sri Lanka|Dena|Sahand'),
('dem_response', r'Democratic response|State of the Union|Gretchen Whitmer|Andy Beshear|Hakeem Jeffries'),
('grok_ai', r'Grok|deepfake|sexual imagery|Dutch court|Netherlands.*AI')]
for label,pat in patterns:
    rx=re.compile(pat,re.I)
    hits=[]
    with path.open(errors='ignore') as f:
        for i,line in enumerate(f,1):
            try: a=json.loads(line)
            except: continue
            dp=str(a.get('date_publish') or '')[:10]
            if dp and dp>'2026-02-13': continue
            blob=(a.get('title') or '')+'\\n'+(a.get('content') or '')
            if rx.search(blob):
                hits.append((i,dp,a.get('source'),a.get('title')))
                if len(hits)>=12: break
    print('\\n--',label,len(hits),'--')
    for h in hits: print(h)
PY"

who were hiding in a forest, opened fire on the police patrols, a police statement said. It said the police killed four

Taliban, also known as Tehreek-e-Taliban Pakistan. Militant violence has been on the rise since the TTP revoked a
ceasefire with Islamabad in late 2022. A suicide bombing killed over 30 people at a mosque in Islamabad on Friday.

- Counting began in Bangladesh’s pivotal national election on Feb 12 after tens of millions of people voted for a new
government following the 2024 ouster of long-time prime minister Sheikh Hasina in a tumultuous Gen Z-driven uprising.
Although final figures were not immediately available, Mr Akhtar Ahmed, senior secretary of the Election Commission,
told reporters that nearly half the electorate had voted at 36,031 of the 42,651 polling centres by 2pm local time, with
2½ hours to go. That is more than the entire 42 per cent turnout at the last election in 2024. Analysts say a decisive
result is crucial for steady governance in the nation of 175 million, as the deadly anti-Hasina protests triggered
months of unrest and disrupted key industries, including the garments sector, the world’s second-largest exporter. It is
the world’s first election after a revolution led by under-30s, or Gen Z, to be followed by Nepal in March. Counting
began at 4.30pm at most booths, immediately after polls closed with early trends expected around midnight and results
likely to be clear by the morning of Feb 13, Election Commission officials said. The contest pits two coalitions led by
former allies, the Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP) and the Islamist Jamaat-e-Islami, with opinion polls giving an
edge to the BNP. Both prime ministerial candidates – the BNP’s Tarique Rahman and Jamaat chief Shafiqur Rahman – said

enthusiasm among the people about the vote,” Mr Tarique Rahman told reporters, while Jamaat’s Mr Shafiqur Rahman called
